name: Join Game
desc: Welcomes the player into the world. Sends entity ID, gamemode, dimension, difficulty, max players, world type, and reduced debug info.

fields.name: maxPlayers
fields.desc: Max players (used to draw tab player list)

fields.name: reducedDebugInfo
fields.desc: Whether to hide coordinates in F3 debug screen

encoding: Player's own entity ID on the server
If true, hides coords from F3
notes: This is the VERY FIRST PLAY-state packet the client receives after login. It creates the WorldClient and EntityPlayerSP. All modules that depend on mc.theWorld or mc.thePlayer being non-null must wait for this packet. It's the universal 'onWorldLoad' trigger.

implementation.overview: S01PacketJoinGame is the first packet received in PLAY state. It creates the client-side WorldClient and EntityPlayerSP, sets the player's entity ID, gamemode, dimension, and difficulty. This packet is the universal trigger for module initialization — modules check `if (mc.theWorld != null && mc.thePlayer != null)` which only becomes true after this packet is processed. Every hacked client uses this packet (or the handleJoinGame method) as their 'onWorldLoad' event trigger. Modules that need the player entity: KillAura, Scaffold, Flight, Speed, ESP, ChestESP, XRay, etc. The entityId field is stored by AntiBot/AntiCheat modules to identify the local player vs other players.

modules.name: SessionInfo / GameInfo Capture
modules.purpose: Capture and display server metadata (gamemode, dimension, difficulty, max players) on the HUD. Also used to trigger module auto-enable on world load.
modules.how_it_works: When S01PacketJoinGame arrives, the module reads all fields (entityId, gamemode, dimension, difficulty, maxPlayers, worldType, reducedDebugInfo) and stores them in a session info data structure. This data is displayed in the HUD (Session Info element), used by the ClickGUI to filter available modules (spectator-only modules, etc.), and fires the client's 'onWorldLoad' event that auto-enables modules.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleJoinGame(S01PacketJoinGame) — creates WorldClient (mc.theWorld) and EntityPlayerSP (mc.thePlayer). After this handler completes, the client has a fully initialized game world. All modules that check mc.theWorld != null can now run.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Anti-cheats don't inspect S01PacketJoinGame — this is a one-time setup packet. However, some anti-bot plugins track how quickly the client sends packets after S01 to detect automated join scripts.

modules.name: BrandDetector / ServerBrand Spoofer
modules.purpose: Detect timing of S01PacketJoinGame to infer server performance (TPS) and detect server software from join response timing.
modules.how_it_works: The time between S02PacketLoginSuccess and S01PacketJoinGame indicates how long the server took to prepare the world for the new player. A long delay suggests high server load. Some modules use this delay to dynamically adjust Timer settings (higher server load = can use higher Timer without detection).
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleJoinGame(S01PacketJoinGame) — the timing between packet arrival and completion of world setup is the key metric.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Server TPS detection from join timing is imprecise (affected by client-side rendering delay). Better TPS detection uses S03PacketTimeUpdate (server ticks) averaged over time.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client-side: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleJoinGame(S01PacketJoinGame) is THE most important handler in the client. It creates WorldClient, EntityPlayerSP, PlayerControllerMP, and MovementInputFromOptions. After this handler completes, the client is in a fully playable state.

In hacked clients, incoming packet events fire:
- Rise 6.0: PacketReceiveEvent
- Jigsaw 0.26: Jigsaw.onPacketRecieved()
- RektSky B5: PacketReceiveEvent
- Aqua 13: EventPacket.RECEIVE + EventReceivedPacket
- Sigma 4.11: EventPacket with isIncoming()
- Memeware 7.3: PacketInEvent

Server-side: Sent by ServerConfigurationManager after the player has been fully initialized (spawn position set, initial inventory items given, etc.).
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: BlinkComponent exempts S01PacketJoinGame from blink buffer. Manager.java watches for world creation as 'onWorldLoad' trigger.
Aqua 13: Backtrack stores joinTime for latency calculations.
Jigsaw 0.26: No module specifically targets S01 — processed through vanilla handler with Jigsaw.onPacketRecieved hook.
RektSky B5: Fire event for module initialization on world load.
Sigma 4.11: EventPacket fires; SessionInfo captures fields for HUD.
